qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Any signed-in user can register for an unpublished conference by calling the public registration endpoint directly
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a conference-registration authorization bypass in `decidim-conferences`, while the advisory is a `Decidim::PrivateExport` ActiveStorage attachment collision in private data exports.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unpublished conference speakers, media, registration metadata, and program endpoints are publicly reachable
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns public access to unpublished conference subresources, not the advisory's UUID-to-bigint collision in private export file attachments.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Import JSON remote URL fields permit low-privilege SSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is SSRF in admin importers that dereference remote URLs, whereas the advisory is a private-export attachment mix-up caused by UUID coercion in ActiveStorage.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the qualifying findings identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a private-data-export leak caused by UUID collisions when `Decidim::PrivateExport` file attachments are stored through an ActiveStorage `record_id` bigint path; the session findings instead cover conference publication/authorization bugs and an import-time SSRF path.
evidence: The advisory describes private export files being attached to the wrong `Decidim::PrivateExport` record because UUID primary keys are coerced through ActiveStorage attachment `record_id` handling.

The `conference_unpublished_registration_bypass` finding targets missing publication/readability checks in public conference registration endpoints and permissions, not private exports or attachment ID coercion.

The `conference_unpublished_subresources_exposure` finding targets conference subresource controllers that authorize child `:list` actions without a publication guard, again unrelated to `Decidim::PrivateExport` or ActiveStorage attachment lookup.
The `import_remote_url_ssrf` finding targets admin importers that dereference attacker-controlled remote URLs, which is a separate component, code path, and vulnerability class from the advisory.
